service_overview.description: User verification service running in AWS VPC, connecting to MongoDB Atlas
service_overview.architecture_summary: Web-based user verification system with AWS VPC hosting, private subnet deployment, and MongoDB Atlas integration
service_overview.assets_to_protect: User credentials, verification data, session tokens, service configurations

scope.assumptions: - TLS encryption for data in transit

spoofing.ai_validation: • ⚠️ Partially Valid: Good identification but needs more context about attack vectors
spoofing.ai_threats:
• Service Identity Spoofing: Attacker impersonating verification service to MongoDB
• DNS Spoofing: Redirecting app.wildOps.com to malicious endpoint
• MongoDB Connection String Spoofing: Intercepting database credentials
spoofing.mitigations:
• Implement mutual TLS authentication between services
• Use AWS IAM roles for service authentication
• Implement strong DNS security controls

tampering.ai_threats:
• MongoDB Data Manipulation: Unauthorized modifications to verification data
• Man-in-the-Middle Attack: Tampering with data between service and MongoDB
• Configuration Tampering: Modification of service security parameters
tampering.mitigations:
• Implement data integrity checks
• Use encrypted connections (TLS)
• Store configurations in AWS Secrets Manager

repudiation.user_threats: • Physical tampering of database server hardware
repudiation.ai_validation: • ❌ Unlikely: Physical access to MongoDB Atlas infrastructure is not a realistic threat
repudiation.ai_threats:
• Audit Log Tampering: Modification of verification service logs
• Transaction Denial: Users denying performed actions
• Database Operation Repudiation: Lack of operation tracking

information_disclosure.ai_validation: • ✅ Valid: Critical security concern for token-based authentication
information_disclosure.ai_threats:
• MongoDB Connection String Exposure
• VPC Configuration Leakage
• Service Logs Exposure

denial_of_service.ai_threats:
• Verification Service Resource Exhaustion
• MongoDB Connection Pool Saturation
• Network Bandwidth Consumption Attack
denial_of_service.mitigations:
• Implement rate limiting
• Set up auto-scaling
• Deploy DDoS protection

elevation_of_privilege.ai_threats:
• Verification Service Role Escalation
• MongoDB Privilege Escalation
• AWS IAM Role Exploitation
elevation_of_privilege.mitigations:
• Implement least privilege principle
• Regular IAM role audits
• Strong role-based access control

raw_analysis:
Based on the provided system architecture, I'll perform a STRIDE threat analysis focusing on the uncovered areas while validating existing threats.

## 1. Service Overview
**Service Name:** User Verification System
**Description:** User verification service running in AWS VPC, connecting to MongoDB Atlas
**Architecture Summary:** Web-based user verification system with AWS VPC hosting, private subnet deployment, and MongoDB Atlas integration
**Assets to Protect:** User credentials, verification data, session tokens, service configurations

## 2. Threat Modeling Scope
**In-Scope Components:** 
- Users interface
- AWS VPC
- Private subnet
- User verification service
- MongoDB Cluster in Atlas Cloud

**Assumptions:** 
- TLS encryption for data in transit
- AWS security groups and NACLs in place
- MongoDB Atlas security features enabled

## 3. STRIDE Analysis

### 3.1 Spoofing Identity
**✓ User Identified Threats**
• Browser-level Redirection Exploitation

**🔍 AI Validation & Opinion**
• ⚠️ Partially Valid: Good identification but needs more context about attack vectors

**🤖 AI Suggested Threats**
• Service Identity Spoofing: Attacker impersonating verification service to MongoDB
• DNS Spoofing: Redirecting app.wildOps.com to malicious endpoint
• MongoDB Connection String Spoofing: Intercepting database credentials

**🛡️ Recommended Mitigations**
• Implement mutual TLS authentication between services
• Use AWS IAM roles for service authentication
• Implement strong DNS security controls

### 3.2 Tampering with Data
**🤖 AI Suggested Threats**
• MongoDB Data Manipulation: Unauthorized modifications to verification data
• Man-in-the-Middle Attack: Tampering with data between service and MongoDB
• Configuration Tampering: Modification of service security parameters

**🛡️ Recommended Mitigations**
• Implement data integrity checks
• Use encrypted connections (TLS)
• Store configurations in AWS Secrets Manager

### 3.3 Repudiation
**✓ User Identified Threats**
• Physical tampering of database server hardware

**🔍 AI Validation & Opinion**
• ❌ Unlikely: Physical access to MongoDB Atlas infrastructure is not a realistic threat

**🤖 AI Suggested Threats**
• Audit Log Tampering: Modification of verification service logs
• Transaction Denial: Users denying performed actions
• Database Operation Repudiation: Lack of operation tracking

### 3.4 Information Disclosure
**✓ User Identified Threats**
• Bearer Token Leakage

**🔍 AI Validation & Opinion**
• ✅ Valid: Critical security concern for token-based authentication

**🤖 AI Suggested Threats**
• MongoDB Connection String Exposure
• VPC Configuration Leakage
• Service Logs Exposure

### 3.5 Denial of Service
**🤖 AI Suggested Threats**
• Verification Service Resource Exhaustion
• MongoDB Connection Pool Saturation
• Network Bandwidth Consumption Attack

**🛡️ Recommended Mitigations**
• Implement rate limiting
• Set up auto-scaling
• Deploy DDoS protection

### 3.6 Elevation of Privilege
**🤖 AI Suggested Threats**
• Verification Service Role Escalation
• MongoDB Privilege Escalation
• AWS IAM Role Exploitation

**🛡️ Recommended Mitigations**
• Implement least privilege principle
• Regular IAM role audits
• Strong role-based access control

## 4. Risk Rating
**Methodology:** High/Medium/Low based on impact and likelihood

**Top Risks Identified:**
1. Bearer Token Leakage (High)
2. MongoDB Connection String Exposure (High)
3. Service Identity Spoofing (Medium)

## 5. Mitigation Plan
**Short-Term Actions:**
- Implement token encryption
- Enable MongoDB Atlas security features
- Deploy AWS WAF

**Long-Term Actions:**
- Implement zero-trust architecture
- Enhanced monitoring and logging
- Regular security assessments
